title: chrysopogon gryllus f. flavescens
intro: Chrysopogon gryllus f. flavescens, also known as the Lemon Grass or Citronella Grass, is a tropical plant belonging to the Poaceae family, native to India, Sri Lanka, and parts of Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and eliminating parasites. The plant is also valued for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the essential oil extracted from the plant is commonly used in aromatherapy, perfumery, and as an insect repellent, while the leaves are used in teas and infusions to promote relaxation and calmness.
